title: libpod: change the default rootless cgroup path parent
body:
user.slice is a poor choice of slice name to put containers in. There is no direct conflict with the system user.slice, but it may still be easily confused by humans and isn't descriptive of its purpose. After user.slice, user-$UID.slice, user@$uid.service, we don't need another user.slice if we can avoid it.

Let's change user.slice to containers.slice to better represent its usage and help disambiguate it from a slew of user cgroups.

there is no particular reason for using `user.slice` so I am ok if we want to change it.

Please adjust all the other occurrences of `user.slice` in the code, documentation and tests
the changes in containers/common must happen in that repository and later vendor the changes in Podman
IS there be a chance the renaming breaks backwards compatibility with previously created/running containers?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

> IS there be a chance the renaming breaks backwards compatibility with previously created/running containers?

I've played locally with this version and it seems to work fine with containers created with an old version of Podman (and the other way around).

I am in favor of this change, the only issue I can imagine of, is that `--cpu-shares` will have a different behavior if there are running containers because `cpu.weight` is relative to its peer cgroups and the old containers have a different parent, I'd not block on this
